text: The upper lobe vein is one of the most difficult structures to be divided through the uniportal approach because of the difficulty to insert the stapler.
In this video we show a simple trick to help the insertion of the staplers and divide the superior pulmonary vein on the right and the left side.
After the accession of the right upper superior pulmonary vein, we use the long curved section as a guide to facilitate the insertion of the stapler to divide this structure.
Thanks to the traction we make with the long curve suction, we create a path for the stapler and we protect the artery from a possible injury.
On the left side we proceed in the same way.
We always recommend to insert the staplers through the anterior part of the incision with angulation.
